channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Could always be worse — 77 replies — 133.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Could always be worse

Open thread • Replies: 77 • Updated: 2026-07-06 12:52 UTC

OP

You could be a German wagie.

>no more AC
>no more sick leave

These are the early signs of a collapsing country. Just wait until the food riots hit.

Early replies (first 3)

62438577
former Blackrock manager is chancellor now, imagine.

62438607
>>62438545
Do you have to send this shit to govt? What if the employer doesn’t give a shit is someone going to audit this?

62438687
>>62438607
If this is anything like Germany's neighbouring country that I live in then the doctor sends it to the government when he inputs it into the system, and then it goes to your employer automatically from there so he knows whether to honour it or not.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62441433
>>62438545
Germans a generally insufferable and completely brainwashed regarding their glorious past. 12 years not a slave.

62441451
>>62441402
>everyone is in on it!
While mostly true there comes a point where there are enemy factions.
Otherwise we would already have a world gov.
Anglosphere still bows to england and so does russia, japan and china.

62441472
>>62441451
Yeah and AfD with it’s obvious russian backing is an enemy faction, no matter how much it claims to be against immigrants and wokeism.

62441478
>>62438545
but i am
and -40% ytd
and fat
and no gf

62441575
>>62441375
For decades we've had this system and everyone was happy. The problem is that the economy is failing because German is very reliant on cheap energy which we've chosen to avoid ever since the ukraine war. If we had pivoted hard into manufacturing renewables or simply having kids we'd be fine.

>>62441427
The reactors we had weren't very good. Nowadays everyone is pro-nuclear because they're thinking of reactor types that weren't even conceptualized 10 years ago. Our problems are that we were reliant on cheap energy which we didn't bother producing and that we have no kids.

62442429
>>62441424
>>62441472
Just fuck off you retard. My relatives even fought against Russia in ww2. I bet YOU are the one not German, or what's your religion I wonder? Let me guess "atheism with Jewish family"? See >>62438768
And don't forget the government is the one prioritizing foreign interests.

62442433
>>62441478
Oooofff at least it's Friday.

62442461
>>62438545
That’s why I let myself be fired, collected unemployment and fucked off. Retarded country full of retarded autism max robots.

62443834
>>62440944
It would be a shame if you coughed into your hands and touched as many public surfaces as possible on the way to the doctor's.

62443931
>>62438545

Both of these are inconceivable to the Australian mind.

62444279
>>62438687
My shithole works this way too, it's ok

62444281
>>62440631
kek

62445477
>>62438545

62445676
>>62441097
what the fuck lol

62445684
the australian painter was right

62446070
>>62445477
Typical kike, gets money thrown at by frens, kikes his way through every bundesracket.

62446094
>>62438770
This problem happens especially when they are kikes(unapologetically evil, subversive and feudal), Germans(stupid, not saying it spitefully, just low IQ outside 1 area per person and zero common sense which ends up in the regulatory clusterfuck Germany and their EU is, energiewende, starting and losing WW2, etc.) or in the case of Merz, stupid AND kike.

62446167
>>62442429
Amazing arguments. Are you seriously denying all the connections AfD has to Putin’s regime? It’s the same shit with alt-right all across europe.

62446771
>>62446167
altrightfags are unironically glazing putin

62446847
>>62446771
Putin is the one good world leader

62447049
>>62438770
Kosher post.

62449791
>>62446847
100RUB ($0,0000000001) have been deposited in your account.

62449805
Merkel destroyed Germany, merz is finishing it off he will start a war with Russia he knows he cant win, the goal being the extermination of all german men just like they did to Ukraine , buy long term puts on the German market they will 1000X

62449815
>>62438545
Kek this is cucked. It's not even the corporation demanding it which makes this even worse.
>you vill work more while the rapefugees live on gibs
Kek imagine living in Germany and having to prove you're ill.

I just remembered they now have rules to prevent you from leaving for a prolonged duration despite the draft not being manadatory lol, lmao even. Oh but only males btw, females exempt.

62450529
>>62446167
I wish they had as much connections as you think. But yes they are a good thing.

62450539
>>62438770
There was no "true right wing party" in Germany that had more then 1% support. So your argument is retarded.

62450552
>>62450539
Even AFD are far leftists isn't their leader a lesbot married to a jeet?

62450633
>>62449805
Merz is an afd agent. He won't start a war with russia.
He will destroy the CDU and then hand the country with a massive military over to the afd which will proceed to invade europe

62450642
>>62450633
Is this what liberals think would happen with the afd? Lol no wonder they are scared shitless

The afd is just another puppet, nothing will change, did any of the other european countries go up in flames after their so called nazi party got into power? Heck some are even worse off with shitskins than before

62450654
>>62450633
Germany has no military

item.title: Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed. — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-07-06 13:24 UTC

OP

Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.

Now many crypto's like XRP and Chainlink are no longer illegal and the banks can start buying it.

Are you ready for the pump of a lifetime in the coming weeks?

Early replies (first 3)

62444894
I just took 70mg of illicit amphetamine sulfate

62444895
>>62444894
That's very nice to hear, I hope you are enjoying yourself.

62444904
>>62444895
it's nothing like meth (oral ROA meth had no euphoria either), minimal vasoconstriction; none really. not euphoric like adderall either, it feels like super non-diuretic caffeine. this has a 1:1 dextro:racemic ratio; so the pharma equivalent is evekeo.

I thought it was bunk but my heart rate is elevated but no arrhythmia like meth (that shit is poison, it'll keep you up for 3-5 days with no perceptual fatigue at significant cost)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62444906
>>62444895
I'm just irritated unless im working or reading something. which is the intended effect, it's just super mechanical caffeine idk how to describe it. it's impossible to "idle" on this shit, I have to be doing something.

62444911
>>62444904
>>62444906
okay well thats nice and all but not really what my thread was about

62444922
>>62444911
hm, do you know how it impacts privacy coins like XMR or even zioncash (ZEC)?

62444929
>>62444922
i do not

62444934
>>62444929
alright, I appreciate it regardless. thoughts on CBDCs?

62444939
>>62444934

I actually dont know much about that also

62444940
>>62444939
you sound like a nice guy no homo

62444948
these 2 got married y'know

62444959
>>62444940
Haha thanks man you too

>>62444948
Nooo we are just friends haha

62444978
>>62444892
> CLARITY ACT
wasn't there some announcement announced for yesterday?

62444991
op they haven't voted on it and aren't close to voting on it. they're hoping to get it done before august 10

62444995
>>62444991
Somone told me clarity act was 4th of july a few weeks ago

62445015
>>62444948
naw, the no homo made marriage impossible
>>62444959
>we are just friends
this

62445147
>>62444978
No, Trump did an interview restating that the US will be the crypto capital of the world.
>>62444892
CLARITY will pass, but there is a lot going behind the scenes that's causing it to stall. Did you know that CLARITY allows banks to buy and sell crypto? The last time US banks traded stocks was before the great depression.

62445160
>>62445147
Oh that's very interesting, thanks for sharing my friend

62445194
So, two more weeks?

62445212
>>62444948
you're responding to a samefag trying to keep his thread at the top because no one cares.

>>62444892
no one cares

62445217
>>62445212
that's not me

62445568
Why isnt crypto pumping harder?

62448360
>>62444892
The banks can already buy both. ETF inflows broke records despite being from small issuers on XRP, highest of any asset on the year, while inflows are borderline non-existent for link.

62448734
Everyone itt thread is a retarded bot.
Fake thread.

62450765
>>62444892
BOOOOOOOOOM

item.title: Index funds are dead — 38 replies — 99.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Index funds are dead

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-07-06 13:03 UTC

OP

Something like 70% of the American public has their money in the stock market.

Index fund investors (“Bogleheads bro!”) want you to believe that they’re all going to be rich because they put $500 a month into the market. This would essentially mean every single American would become rich just by DCAing into the US stock market.

Index investing is dead. The ONLY reason it worked in the past is because like 5% of Americans owned stocks. The ones that got in first benefited from everyone else piling in, exactly like Bitcoin.

Look at Bitcoin, all the Bitcoin retards made the same mistake of telling people it’ll just go up and up forever, now we’ve plateaued for the last 5 years and the Bitcoin trade is dead.

The only winners going forward will be good active investors. You’ve been warned.

Early replies (first 3)

62448308
It's all going to crash in the next 5 years, and economic pain will be unlike anything we've ever felt. So enjoy your gay stocks you fucking wealthsimple zoomer nigger.

62448320
>>62448306
It's so over for America saar! India superpower by 2030!

62448334
>>62448306
You're forgetting that since the 1970s and the birth of the Internet we've seen basically all major corporations become multi-national entities, access to the American markets overseas increasing, sovereign wealth funds worth trillions of dollars coming up with holding timelines that basically go to infinity since they're not constrained to the lifespan of a single person, etc.
The days of the USA markets being constrained to just America is fucking over. That's why the Buffett Indicator is such a worthless metric now. The USA has basically conquered the globe in terms of stocks, sure you'll get some thirdies teeth gnashing over this and crying about BRICS but fundamentally the Boglechads are still right and are still early.
>B-BUT THAT MEANS AMERICANS WITH STOCKS ARE GOING TO BE WEALTHY
Yeah, no shit. We're the best country on earth, of course we're wealthy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62448407
>>62448397
Ok and? Ur monopoly money is better than mine, gj saaar!

62448419
>>62448306
What is the relevance of your picrel?

62448444
North afrimerican glamour is diminishing, starcucks just ain't that cool anymore

62448451
>>62448407
I'll accept your concession :)

62448604
Fixed mortgages are the reason these hyperinflation dollar is worthless scenarios are such a pipe dream.

62448645
Cool story bro, still buying $250/week though

62448657
>>62448306
>Index fund investors (“Bogleheads bro!”) want you to believe that they’re all going to be rich because they put $500 a month into the market.

We can wiggle with the definition of rich but if you start young yes you'll have millions of dollars by retirement. If you consider people who are actually making decent money and able to put away $1000, $5000, $10k a month it's undeniable.

>This would essentially mean every single American would become rich just by DCAing into the US stock market.

Yes.

>Index investing is dead.

Why is it dead?

>now we’ve plateaued for the last 5 years and the Bitcoin trade is dead.

You sound like a pretty tarded zoomer. If a market ever drops to a 5 year low then it's over? What the fuck is a recession or depression to you?

62448707
>>62448306
>the tulip market failed this proves that index funds are dead
So how's the weather in Karachi, Mahboub?

62448715
Quitting my job was a mistake. It wasn't a good job, but getting yourself hyped up to apply for and get a new job and commute to work is a pain in the ass. It's easier to stick with routine. If I joined the military in 2017 after working at the ice cream shop due to my worthless degree I could have slipped into some meme clearance job working with Servicenow or something for the last 3 years once they stopped caring about vaccines. In 2022 and 2023 I could have just been on unemployment, severance pay, going back to school with military help.

62448739
>>62448715
For a loser the military is a great opportunity to acquire wealth

62448751
>>62448739
Kelly's Heroes was fictional though and I'm tired of pretending it wasn't.

62448752
>>62448339
>taking market risk just to break even

He does it for free!

62449026
>>62448339
It's 7% a year in the future but 5% a year in reality adjusted for inflation

62449374
>>62448308
>t. Doomer from 5 years ago

62449868
So whats better?

62449870
After inflation and taxes, you get like 1-2% from index funds

Its fucking retarded

62449878
>>62448306
>The only winners going forward will be good active investors.

So basically just trading firms with proprietary digital superintelligence who farm everyone else for exit liquidity.

Is it possible, is it just possible, that the average person is screwed, just like they were for 99.999999% of history?

62449882
>>62448306
I was actively stock picking for the past year and I did beat the market but I’m too lazy to constantly be researching and listening to my cabal of financial pundits everyday.

I’m just gonna DCA my paycheck into VOO and a bit of VXUS and let it be. I’m not willing to work harder or hustle. So if this doesn’t work that’s too bad Sonny

62449891
the only way to beat the market is buying bitcoin or leveraged bitcoin at the bottom, the rest is gambling

62449899
>>62449870
>taxes

Found the dumb incel crybaby who hasn't read the tax code

62450040
>>62448306
>70% of Americans have money in index funds
>index funds are dead
"No one goes there anymore, it's too crowded"

62450136
The real reason index funds are dying is because bailing out and enshrining the monopolies of the top companies in every index is no longer and will no longer be a popular investment strategy when Israel wields trump over Iran having some uranium and there won't be an occupy wall Street idpol smokescreen to bail out the AI bubble popping that is only what it is because of east Asian ram fan plants being extremely mafioso with how they deal to the mag7 who only survive by perpetuating the myth that the minimum wage rising is good for the economy and not a root cause of evaporating the middle class with inflation. If you get laid off because of ai you are not investing in an AI bubble boondoggle index fund that is 20%+ Nvidia, msft, AAPL, goog, meta, pltr, intc etc.... you are investing bear

62450142
I'm gonna get so fucking rich from my investments, I'm so lucky money wise.

62450144
>>62450142
You eatin good soon bro, donchu worry bout it :)

62450343
>>62448334
Holy shit that literal 30 year crab market from 1970 to 2000.

That couldn't happen again... right bros..?

The whole "10% average return per year over the last 100 years" thing wasn't calculated by taking into account the massive asset bubble of the last 5 years... was it bros...?

62450420
>>62448366
shut the fuck up you dumb pajeet

62450553
>>62450343
You didn't actually read the caption of the chart did you, speed-reader-kun?

62450666
>>62448334
> We're the best country on earth, of course we're wealthy.

62450677
>>62448752
literally anything else carries exponentially higher risk.

>>62449026
if you believe the government figure for inflation you really are the best goy

62450682
>>62449870
LTCG ensures if you only pull out 120k a year (in profit for married couples only) you'll pay no federal tax.

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 28 replies — 90.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-07-06 13:08 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62449998
>>62449989
So you were just playing video games all that time? I would train and sign up for the military or something.

62450000
>>62449998
kys

62450003
>>62449998
Even if I wasn't past enlistment age that's retarded. Jumping straight into military trading would probably kill me.

62450007
>>62450003
It's one of zogbots who recommend military to everyone

62450019
get a haircut, shave your beard, get a nice suit, remember to sleep well for 1-2 weeks before the interview, always smile and say thank you.

62450028
Your best bet is make money online

62450080
>>62450028
Computers cost money I don't have.

62450086
>>62450080
How are you getting food and other expenses paid for now?

62450088
>>62450007
It would solve your depression and incelisms

62450098
>>62450086
Magic.
>>62450088
Stop posting.

62450099
>>62450098
I was going to try and help but no, stay poor faggot

62450102
>>62450099
Liar

62450111
It's too late for you OP, you missed your opportunity. AI, robots and immigrants are making labor go to zero. Meaning no more jobs for you.

62450116
If you're in the US travel to mexico, and then from there travel to asia or something, broaden your horizons before some disabled downy recruiter manages to convince you to turn yourself into cannon fodder\fertilizer for shlomo

62450127
This isn't even trolling, you're just a bitter asshole trying to pick on someone to make yourself feel better. You're a loser.

62450160
>>62449961
There are no skill jobs such as security guard, food delivery etc. Try applying to those.

62450206
>>62449961
You'd better hope you're a trans black retarded disabled vet then. Otherwise it's doordash and walmart greeter until you die.

This is the endgame for all NEETs. What did you think what going to happen?

62450220
>>62450206
It's OK you'll lose everything soon like most north cuckmericans and you'll both be equal (broke), except you spent all your time on a hamster wheel

62450437
Too bad I can't get serious posters. Just one or two attempted trolls.

62450440
>>62450437
Go apply for a job then retard why are you still here

62450478
>>62450440
It's 4 in the morning, fuckface.

62450482
Just make up a story you worked for your parents business or something the last 20 years and now they retired and sold it and you need a job. Don’t walk into McDicks and tell them you haven’t done shit in 20 years.

62450490
>>62450437
You are entitled manchild. You got your answers. Entry level jobs or army. Those are your only options beside killing yourself faggot. That is what your get for over 20 years of parasiting on your parents lazy nigger

62450558
>>62449961
Any job that requires an application like your pic you are pretty fucked unless you can find a way to lie on it, otherwise seek jobs without an application, plenty of ads on Craigslist for stuff like construction work or simple labour jobs. For the most part you just call the listings and as long as you sound normal, motivated and willing to work asap, they will have you on for a test day. They may ask to meet on site 1st for a quick in person interview before hiring but most of the time they just do the phone interview, they may ask question about experience, you will have to lie and say you’ve done general labour work in the past and can complete any non skilled labour no problem. Once you get your test day then you just have to not be the slowest guy on the job site and you will get the job

62450695
So, none of you know what you're talking about? I've got one guy crying bitch tears because he realized he wasted years of his life working minimum wage for nothing and another guy actually recommending Craigslist odd jobs.
Holy Christ on high, we're all screwed but at least I'm not as screwed as you!

item.title: THE cuckolds of crypto — 22 replies — 88.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
THE cuckolds of crypto

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-07-06 13:25 UTC

OP

>2021
$53

>2026
$7

Early replies (first 3)

62447356
yep

62447358
>turkish peeler laughing at anyone
Where's your wife and kids Adem?

62447360
>>62447358
Not Adem

He won btw, cuckolds lost (check the price)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62447380
>>62447360
You fuddies look all the same to me. Mentally ill brownoids.

62450196
>>62447380
you're poor lol

62450229
>>62447360
Using Ronaldo as a reaction image carries a 99% chance you are from a 3rd world country. Good luck with the IBS and coke addiction though.

62450242
>>62450229
>Lyrica (pregabalin) is a prescription medication used to treat nerve pain, epilepsy (seizures), generalized anxiety disorder, and fibromyalgia. It works by calming overactive nerves in the brain and spinal cord.
>Common side effects include dizziness, drowsiness, and weight gain
>generalized anxiety
>weight gain
>IBS
Can't make this shit up. EVERY FUCKING TIME fuddies accidentally leak any perosnal info, it's some extremely embarrassing shit about their disgustingly depressing lives. Another board spamming LINK fuddie added to the rather long list of fat, drug addicted mentally ill losers that latched onto Chainlink to cope with the bleakness of their pathetic existence.

62450257
>>62450229
>>62450242
Addiction and link fudders is truly a combo for the ages.

https://warosu.org/biz/thread/62309887#p62310297
>yeah bro way too much, i was addicted to Lyrica back in 2018. I still have a psychological addiction to it (along with cocaine since it goes hand in hand). The withdrawals are horrible but on the bright side they don't last a lot and the tolerance drops quickly. Be careful man!

62450271
>>62450229
fucking hell imagine the life of a fudder. its like that brazilian creep that posts daily about how "young" he looks while he strides in the crumbling streets of the favela where he lives

only to return to the shack, take his gut pills and fud LINK for 6hrs a day kek

62450273
>>62450257
>cocaine addiction
is this 'apeyieldgod' again? are all link fudders literal porn, alcohol and heavy drug addicts?

62450320
>>62450271
>>62450273
It's bleak. This guy is responsible for at least half of all LINK fud threads. Apart from the fat AI slob pic threads and 2021 vs 2026 price threads (like this one), and "Adem won" threads, he is also the CMC price screenshot OP, like >>62425872. He posted a Ronaldo gif in that thread which is also used in many other fud threads with the same image name.

I quickly whipped up a collage as this shit is yet again too funny, hope it's readable enough.

62450427
>>62450320
he also once "bragged" about paying for prostitutes (that he also stole from). he even admitted that he is 36 years old and financially unstable in that same thread. too lazy to find it in the archives but it is incredibly obvious, even without knowing any of this, that this dude is beyond pathetic

62450473
>>62447354
Anybody here remember the summer of dreams?

62450483
>>62450473
crushed by Bitcoin

62450531
>>62450473
I remember the summer of dreams like it was yesterday. What a time to be alive bros

62450646
>>62450531
What is your addiction, anon? Feel free to share, seems like your fellow fuddies are not afraid to lay it all bare. Porn, alcohol, benzos, cocaine, Lyrica. What is your poison?

62450663
>>62450320
Lol don't forget their fuddie twitter groupchat mascot is a mudslime third worlder who is obsessed with nigger dicks
Probably comes from being exposed to that qur an story at a young age, the one about those al zutt niggers passing mohammed around like a communal fleshlight until he limped back to his followers the next day

62450669
can someone explain to me why link retards gloat and ridicule le fuddies when their shitcoin is in the gutter? is this delusion, coping mechanism, or plain retardation?

62450698
>>62450669
It's just bear market banter anon, don't let it get to you so bad. We're all LINK holders here, fuddies included. They shit on LINK so they can feel vindicated when it goes down, securing some dopamine even when they are losing money. It's like betting against your favorite sports team so you still "win" when they lose, except in this case they lose money when they win. Capiche?

62450716
>>62450242
>>62450257
>>62450273
>>62450271
based Link fudders dabbling with fun drugs

Meanwhile cuckolds are poor incels living in mommy's basement because their investment turned out to be a disaster and think drugs are LE BAD or something

62450758
>>62450698
the state of bagholders reading comprehension

62450769
>>62450758
Link cuckols are insane and think anyone who mocks Link actually holds that shitcoin. Typical 3rd world mentality (most Linkies are thirdies btw)

item.title: my brother-in-law is a literal genius, like 150+ IQ, and worked his ass off over the last 15 years or so to become a suc… — 36 replies — 70.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
my brother-in-law is a literal genius, like 150+ IQ, and worked his ass off over the last 15 years or so to become a suc…

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-07-06 12:56 UTC

OP

my brother-in-law is a literal genius, like 150+ IQ, and worked his ass off over the last 15 years or so to become a successful entrepreneur and just completely failed. he's flat broke now. it's crazy to me how hard it is to make good money even if you're smart and hardworking. the world is fucking brutal.

Early replies (first 3)

62449875
>>62449873
“Smart” and didn’t go all in crypto. I shiggy diggy

62449876
Almost like being a business owner is just being a wagie with more work and all of your capital at risk.

62449881
>>62449876
Nah that's being self employed, an entrepreneur convertes other peoples time and labour into money

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62449909
More white incel loser cope

>waaaah whatever i could do is too hard
>waaah what if i dont instantly succeed
>waaah i deserve everything with no effort
>waaaah boomers got everything easy waaaaaah

62449923
>>62449873
Its probably because he's not that smart.

62449926
>>62449909
this is also true. relying on extrinsic outcome motivation is a 100% guaranteed way to an perma incel doomloop with the end-stage being suicide

62449942
>>62449909
Name one successful poojeet franchise

62449948
>>62449942
This is more white incel loser cope

>waaaah anyone who calls out my crybaby behavior is an Indian, a foid or a joo

Grow up

62449954
>>62449873
If he gives up after one failure he's not going to make it through the next 5 failures that will eventually teach him how to succeed

also geniuses don't usually do great at business. The sweet spot for starting a business is in the 100-120 range. Smart enough to make good decisions but dumb enough not to give up when things go wrong anyways.

62449984
>>62449948
Lol poojeet fails to name a brand and seethes

62449992
>>62449984
Not him but that one pajeet was smart with providing Indians with free internet which then destroyed the internet for the rest of the world.

62449994
>>62449992
Steve poojobs? He did look a little bit indian at the end

62450060
>>62449873
He's probably too smart. He want to create great things that revolutionize peoples lives, but the easiest way to make it is to scam stupid people.

62450077
>>62449984
Why would a poojeet take orders from a white incel loser? Y'all lower caste than dalits. Know your place and lower your tone

62450101
>>62449873
I got paid a full day at my job on Thursday for LITERALLY doing nothing but writing my magnum opus novel, watching my niece and playing pokemon showdown.


It's a skill issue. Not an IQ one.

62450119
>>62449876

If you work “in” the business it’s just like owning your job.

62450121
>>62449942

Tata motors which owns Jaguar and Land Rover.

62450123
>>62450101

You have a job tho, it’s different than trying to start a business

62450129
>>62449887
top silicon valley ceos are all high iq, top wallstreet executives too, there's just no way you can survive that kind of environment without constantly observing obscure patterns, so your shallow analysis doesn't track
>>62449873
your brother in law probably failed because he wasn't part of the group, he probably tried to muscle into someone else's turf, what business was he into?

62450177
>>62450121
I know of someone who own a land rover, a newer model. He went for a drive in sand with it and the engine burned out, he got it replaced and went down to the beach to drive over sand dunes. Engine burned out again

Lmao aryan quality, I didn't know tata acquired land rover in 2008 but it makes sense now

62450181
>>62450077
You took your vaccines like a good cattle everyone knows you poojoos are the most subservient minions

62450429
>>62450077
Castes aren't a real thing it's some turdjeet larp among themselves.
In reality there's only turdjeets and actual human (white) people.

62450441
>>62450429
Face it you white incel losers are the lowest caste people in the world. Unlike Indians NO ONE likes you or wants you around AND you are BROKE

62450451
>>62450441
Get off that white dick poopoid

62450460
>>62450451
Post some more about how you are broke and depressed but deserve an easy life because you are white

62450481
>>62450460
I deserve an easy life and I have an easy life while you're broke and depressed and looking for some dick

62450485
>>62449873
I'm retarded and have made millions.
Just the way it goes sometimes OP.
Being smart isn't a ticket to success crazy enough.

62450500
>>62449873
BTDT. I was lucky that Bitcoin came along when it did or I'd be living in a cardboard box sucking off meth addicts.

62450584
>>62449873
>150+ IQ
>worked his ass off over the last 15 years
158 IQ here. Only one of those statements can be true (probably the 2nd). If he were actually that smart he'd have known how to optimize things so he didn't need to work his ass off. I've been loafing about the last few decades and my liquid net worth is just over $4m having barely lifted a finger.

62450615
>>62450584

This is where we mention there is autistic "intelligent", and then there is intuition. Some people can solve anything you put in front of them but can't inductively synthesize a shrewd or creative idea. AI brain, basically lol.

62450621
>>62449887
High IQ overthink, underact, avoid risk taking
>AAAHH! I have tooo thiiiiiink first
Low IQ underthing, overact and dont even register risk

62450636
>>62450584
Wanna invest in my super secret club?

62450659
Naybe OP isn't.smart enough to recognize whether somebody os a genius. What if the brother in law is actually a dumb ass and has people fooled?

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 134 replies — 62.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 134 • Updated: 2026-07-06 11:17 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445705
>>62445700
im not even a shill, as i also contemplate the complications of the CUSIP Conundrum. i even emails Cusip Global Services about it in March and they never got back to me--how very curious. that said, i am so sick of these retards on twitter pretending like they're doing anything but peddling false hype. they don't even bring up the CUSIP-6 thing which is fucking insane to me because that is a piece of evidence staring us right in the face for months now which has very interesting implications.

62445706
>>62445686
ABC? You mean Alfred?

62445735
>>62445619
relax.
He does software engineering (hes actually been stuck in tech support for 6 years).

He can hack you (him hacking you is just making shit up and alphastate reposting it)

62445743
>>62445686
Jake already confirmed we won, so it doesn't matter what anyone else says.

62445859
>>62445743
did anyone else itt literally cum when they saw that picture of him on the couch

62445900
>>62445859
Yea but like, most of us cum every day at least.

62445941
>>62445705
A silly shilly
Being a seethy weethy
Built for GME

62446010
>>62445859
I'm going to frame the picture of Jake on the couch and put it up in my mansion. When guests will ask who he is, I'll smirk and say "you wouldn't get it."

62446088
>>62445569

62446106
>>62445576
>>62445600
>>62445645

spot on

62446149
>>62446088
They leave trading open so smart people can sell "for tax purposes" and get the loss on their tax return. I guess they think idiots buying 7 biĺion worthless securities for 8 cents each is funny,,,,,i do

62446238
>>62445569
what is this image even from?

62446242
>>62446238
wait I found it:

https://www.timesofisrael.com/us-hockey-team-owner-suspended-after-social-media-spat-with-fan-over-gaza-war/

HOLY SHIT ITS FROM 2024. Imagine what him seething at RC has done to him since?

62446288
>>62446242
Hes 60 years old whats the joke here?

62446344
>>62446242
<suspended


Lmao so nothing happened to him except a meaningless press release BBBYbbqers cant stop losing

62446386
>>62446344
the real fun starts when the rico roundup is in full swing. But even better is when cash and new equity is distributed to previous BBBYQ holders.

Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

62446441
>>62446386
>Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

Your $0 stays $0

62447283
CUSIP-6

62447659
Ryan Cohen confirmed AGAIN that bathroom gods have won by posting on X that shows he has ties to the Icahn family. Shills will immediately come out of the woodwork now that I bring this up. It's over shills, we won. It's been 100% confirmed.

62447686
>>62447659
it's not brett icahn's fiance the birthmarks don't match

62447756
Baggie general

62447918
>>62447756

62448131
lamestop holders are seething because they missed out on the true moass. bathroom gods are the chosen ones. they trusted the plan above all. the cancellation of shares was the ultimate IQ test. weaker people couldn't handle that. now they are filled with regret.

62448146
>>62448131
The silo around BBBY was truly impressive. Superstonk made no mention of it. /GME/ was a full blown silo as well. It wasn't until I looked at the option chain on BBBY and saw the insane volatility that made me look in to it further. Greatest decision of my life.

62449186
>>62449050
all of them are short bbbyq. i guarantee it

62449344
>>62449050
>>62449186
If you are assassinated, does your short position get closed? Asking for Brian Thompson

62449448
>>62449344
Nope. His estate would get liquidated to pay off to buy back the shares.

62450370
CUSIP-6

62450461
>>62448146
The silo around eBay has become palpable

Y
G
O

62450559

62450582

62450610

62450630

meh, could be worse. i think the 32 coins really did innoculate the market

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 110 replies — 38.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 110 • Updated: 2026-07-06 10:34 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442227
>>62439167
But that won't help the price up

62442228
>>62442214
Now???

62442255
>>62426512
I dca 60% of my savings each month while saving the remaining 40% for potential lower lows as we approach October, at which point I'll start going in with all of my monthly savings and that 40% I've been holding back.

62442503
>>62442227
Maybe not immediately. But without infrastructure, there's nothing to drive long-term value anyway.

62442507
>>62442214
Yup, little by little.

62442511
>>62442507
>>62442228
I dollar cost. THe past few months have been gr8

62442553
>>62442511
Where do you do that

62442563
>>62426512
I am DCAing in

62442650
>>62442503
memecoins pump faster. I made money on Ansem and some Solana gems

62442655
>>62439758
never trust centralised services

62442658
buy btc goys

62442668
>>62442658
oy vey

62443383
>>62442650
Well, that's good for you.
>>62442655
Same. Self-custody first, then I check if the protocol has had multiple audits.

62443384
>>62442658
Do it man!

62443930
>>62442668
Goy

62444179
I'm waiting for October, but if it goes below 55k I'll probably start slurping. Everyone who says crypto I'd dead is retarded. Not ripping = dead is their logic.

62444587
>>62426537
That's never going to happen, max will be around 45K. My plan is to DCA, I got in around 58K, stake a few on babylon, I'm waiting to see if there will be more dump

62444608
>>62444587
You really trust the Babylon protocol? It's my first time looking into it and I see I could earn 0.02+ BTC every month. $1200+ every month sounds much better than sitting in my Ledger.

62444998
>>62444608
really

62445005
>>62426512
I'm selling at 100k. I've had enough of holding and should have sold last year. I wanna get out of this shit, it's too much stress.

62445018
>>62427357
do not pull

62445689
>>62444998
DYOR

62446816
>>62445018
Who's plebs rn?

62447133
>>62445005
regrets?

62447140
Ngmi

62447522
>>62442255
Just keep going

62448616
>>62426537
this is probably correct : (

trump and the political class already made their money off crypto last couple years

62448797
>>62427359
Surely the bottom would be zero, no?

62449904
>>62448797
people like to imagine when theyre on drugs

62450375
Why

item.title: When the FUCK is this AI shit going to crash? — 20 replies — 30.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
When the FUCK is this AI shit going to crash?

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-07-06 12:47 UTC

OP

When the FUCK is this AI shit going to crash?

Early replies (first 3)

62444975
Don’t cry Bobina

62445016
>>62444969
Oooooooooo BOOOOOOOBIIIINNNNAAAAAAAA OOOOOOOOOOOOHHHHHHHHUUUUUUUHHHHHH!!!!!!!!!!!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445048
>>62444969
55k next weeek!

62445049
bobina is thick asf

62448136
Comforting sex with Bobina

62448224
>>62444969
i unironically think it is too big to fail
>if it crashes the US economy is fucked
>elites are depending on it for their mass datacenter surveillance state
>if US companies fail then chyna wins
US government will prop it up at all costs
prove me wrong

62448245
>>62444969
Unironically soon.

62448465
>>62444969
when you FOMO in

62448519
bobina dev is a deranged individual, stay away at all costs

62449610
>>62444969
IDK enough about the finances of the companies to say, but the demand for the hardware isn't going away any time soon. If you need hardware, buy now. When M5 studio drops, it's probably going to be up to 128gb of ram, and those will sell out in a heartbeat, further driving up prices on RTX 6000's and DGX sparks when people panic-buy whatever significant compute they can.

62449618
>>62444969
that's hard to predict. in the end, Ed Zitron will be proven right.

62449622
>>62444969
Bobina best grill

62449659
>>62448519
hello chudcel

62449668
Bobos in shambles

62449893
>>62444969
H-h-help me leave my short cleanly!!

62450137
>When the FUCK is this horseless carriage shit going to crash?

This is how retarded you sound OP

62450141
>>62450137
>car: obviously useful for transportation
>llm: generates naked cartoon bears

62450151
I honestly think that the crash won’t really change shit until 5+ years after. The stock for future batches of ram and ssd are already bought up until 2029 (and will probably be gone for more batches in the future). And while slop will definitely be reduced, it will still be slop and companies will realize that ai shit isn’t good, and the job market will still be like shit (tho it won’t get much worse)

62450641
>>62444969
>spaceX going public to save X AI
>anthropic going public soon
>openai thinking about going public
>all of them spending out the ass on capex
>increasing reports from other companies that AI hasn’t been all it was cracked up to be

They’re literally going public to use us for exit liquidity. The valuations are such that the large-cap index funds will have to buy substantial stakes upon listing, and of course retail investoors will gobble it up trying to make a quick profit or thinking they’re “early.” I give it a year or so but it’s going to pop

item.title: Post country and how much PTO you get >aus >5 weeks holiday 2 weeks sick — 63 replies — 18.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Post country and how much PTO you get >aus >5 weeks holiday 2 weeks sick

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-07-06 10:05 UTC

OP

Post country and how much PTO you get
>aus
>5 weeks holiday 2 weeks sick

Early replies (first 3)

62443952
US
"Flexible" but I took seven weeks last year

62443958
>>62443952

If you’re white collar professional is the USA really a nightmare for employees like everyone says ? Or it’s over exaggerated?

62443972
>>62443958
Depends on the company I guess. I've always imagined that your average retard that complains about a lack of PTO works fast food, retail, or something equally menial

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62447310
Notice how America is right in-between or on par with most European countries, yet the doomer grifters on YouTube are always pushing shit about how Americans don't get any days off.

Same with the retarded air-conditioning debate. Now that Europeans are actually visiting Florida and Texas, they're like "oh, sorry Americans, we didn't realize you live in a fucking jungle hellscape".

62447320
>ITT: extreme lying and coping Amerifats

62447342
>>62443950
>CZ
>none, because I'm a business owner
>used to be 4-6 weeks + some sick days + national holidays when I was an employee
>used to be unlimited paid holiday when I worked for a certain gamedev studio as a contractor

62447350
>>62443950
Thank god I'm no longer a wagie but I used to get 27 days PTO which was actually only 20-21 after you account for holidays when the pharmacy was closed anyway. I had to work for 8 years to get to that point. Years 1 to 7 mean you get 5 fewer days. On top of that, there were a bunch of gay rules for when and how time off could get approved. I used to have to swap shifts with a bunch of other pharmacists if I wanted 3 weeks off in a row for international travel.

62447370
>>62447300
>>62447301
I'm in USA

I get 25 - 30 days of paid vacation. If I included Holidays it would be another 10 days. And I'm private sector. Government sector or financial/banking employees get even more holidays than that.

Quit with the seething.

62447386
>>62444126
>USPS
Were federal but independent
>wounded warrior leave 13 days
>annual leave 20 days/ 26 if you've been here like 15 years or something
>sick leave 13 days
>federal holidays are off

62447414
>>62447370
>>62447320
Curious.
I worked for American companies for decades. Northrop Grumman. Booz Allen. GD etc.
Mostly I had 10 to 15 days total PTO and 0 sick leave. With a small company I got up to 22 days total PTO after 5 years tenure.

62447425
>>62447414
Yes, it's based on years of service. Which makes sense. Some loser shouldn't be able to get hired, work for 1 month, take 2 weeks for, get paid, then quit.

62447617
Another amerifat here. I work in biotech. 4 weeks vacation, plus 1 week sick time, but were discouraged from using that unless we’re “””actually sick.””” Fed holidays too, plus they give everyone the week between Christmas and new years off. After one more year there I’ll get 5 weeks vacation.

62447636
>>62447320
People on /biz/ are generally more successful then your average wagie thus better benefits with their job. Your average wagie that talks about stocks is piling into something that already had parabolic growth and is due for a drawdown. Their PoV is worthless

62447666
>>62443950
5 weeks. American but yuro company.

>>62443958
Yes it's one reason I haven't changed jobs. 2 weeks is normal even for good jobs.

62447893
>>62447310
Yeah reading this thread was genuinely surprising. I finally visited Europe (Italy) for the first time over the last 2 weeks and while it was very nice, I don't think life in europe is way better than life in America like I was led to believe. They each have advantages and disadvantages. But with that said, it is a lot easier to accrue personal wealth here than there.
>>62447301
Yeah, I didn't count holidays either even though they're technically PTO.
>>62447298
Doesn't everyone have unlimited sick leave on paper at least? We're only talking about PTO.

62447964
>>62447893
I mean I have 4 kids and when they get sick the pediatrician writes me a note that gives me a week paid off work.

62447974
>>62447964
I can do the same. For up to 4 weeks i don't even need the note.

62447987
>>62447964
>>62447974
Oh, but after 4 weeks i do need the note and it stops being paid.

62447990
>>62447281
How many dicks did you have to suck to get your stellar reputation?

62448091
>>62447974
>>62447987
If I wasn't American I'd probably believe you but alas.

62448115
>>62448091
It's true. My manager is very relaxed about sick leave because he himself uses it every year.

62448199
>>62448115
/thread

Never once in my 15 years of work have I had a manager deny, or get pissed about me using my vacation days. Like I said earlier, this is all doomer nonsense people watch on YouTube or read on left-wing news headlines.

And the Americans that don't get paid leave (like waiters), have open work schedule, unlike factory workers. Waiters can take off or trade shifts with people whenever they want, they just don't get paid for it.

62448201
>>62447617
/thread

62448206
>>62444410
>Also how does it work if you leave the company? Here we get paid out our leave (not sick tho) in cash when we quit.
It's different state by state. In most states, companies don't have to pay out your vacation bank if you leave, but every time I had that I found the company was willing to pay it out.
On the unlimited side that means there's no bank to pay out, but once you get to that level it doesn't make much of a difference unless you leave willingly (at which point you probably got a signing bonus at the new job anyway) or if you get laid off you get a severance package, or if you were fired for misconduct or not doing your job they weren't going to pay it out anyway.

62448231
>>62447281
>nigger slave bragging about job security in the 1800s

62448269
i get 22 days + 12 company holidays + half day fridays in the summer. i don't know how many sick days i get, but i wfh so it's not a big deal.

62448433
In Denmark, PTO is handled through a national accrual tax systems rather than being fully granted upfront.

Standard full time employee:
>~2.08 vacation days per month worked (25 days/year)

I work for a tech company that offers extra 5 days:
>~0.42 vacation days per month worked (5 days/year)
>allowed to borrow up to 5 days

I can effectively use up to 35 days of PTO/year, but ofc I'm then at -5 so I'd have to work like 2 months just to get back to 0 PTO. If I were to quit I'd simply get less salary at the end.

Also, 1 PTO day for moving and unlimited sick days. I work an average of 30-35 hours a week.

62448461
UK private sector, 25 days of holiday, 8 bank holidays. We don't get allocated sick days, you just stay off work when you're sick. People really game the system, I'd guess the average person takes four or five sick days a year.

62448490
>>62443950
USA 2 weeks total sick/personal time. Please just put me out of my misery.

62448500
>>62443950
>Post country and how much PTO you get
4 weeks annual leave, will go up to 5 weeks in a few years. 3 weeks sick leave. I can carry over up to 6 weeks of annual leave year to year and can carry over infinite sick leave into the pool.

>>62443958
>If you’re white collar professional is the USA really a nightmare for employees like everyone says ? Or it’s over exaggerated?
Most people cry over nothing IMO but it depends on employer, some really are as bad as they as say, some are substantially better than anything you'll find in Europe too (tech, Healthcare mostly). For leave, I'd say 2-3 weeks of PTO total (both sick and annual leave) is common for 20-30 year olds as a professional for a corporation but it does go up generally with years of service. You will absolutely make significantly more than 95% of Europeans in a similar position with (potentially) a lower cost of living but will likely have to work more than 40 hours a week. I personally would rather make more money, take risks, and retire early so the US system is far superior to the European model but it depends on what value, Euros tend to value guarantees and low risk far more than I do.

62450314
>>62443950
canada
20 days vacation
11 days holidays
10 days sick

62450317
>>62443950
>Post country
uk
>and how much PTO you get
none i run my own business

62450326
>>62444108
What's the pay there? What projects do you usually work on?

item.title: How much BTC would be enough for you? — 7 replies — 9.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How much BTC would be enough for you?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-07-06 12:40 UTC

OP

Be honest, anon. What's your number?

>0.1 BTC? 1 BTC? 10 BTC?
Or does the goal just keep moving every time you reach it?

Early replies (first 3)

62450402
>>62450395
Road to 16btc

62450405
>>62450395
0.00001

62450407
Buy ethereum

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450449
>>62450395
i could buy 0.1
what i dont do
i will probably hate myself in 10years

62450554
>>62450402
that's good for you

62450561
Zero

62450627
>>62450395
3 fiddy

item.title: Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news? — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news?

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-07-06 13:24 UTC

OP

Seems like every BTC pump or dump is blamed on ETF flows or macro headlines.

>Is that just the reality now, or are people overlooking other factors?

Early replies (first 3)

62450753
>>62450728
Its 100% cz dependent, just tradingview has to write something to the price action.

62450760
>>62450728
Btc does this thing where it lulls the newfag into a false sense of superiority. Both in bull and bear markets. In a bull market, it tricks newfags into thinking it will go up forever and traps them in overleveraged longs and in bear markets, it tricks them into it never going back up and they either sell low, sell covered calls or lever up short and get milked. Just hold spot and don't pay attention for a decade.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Now that I've made it I'm wondering if it's worth dumping $6,000 on having a foursome with 3 high class STD-tested escor… — 5 replies — 7.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Now that I've made it I'm wondering if it's worth dumping $6,000 on having a foursome with 3 high class STD-tested escor…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-07-06 12:46 UTC

OP

Now that I've made it I'm wondering if it's worth dumping $6,000 on having a foursome with 3 high class STD-tested escorts.

Surely there's other rich anons here that have played out that fantasy? How was it? Anything you'd do differently if you had to do it again?

This is financial advice since although I won't miss that amount of money on a once-in-a-lifetime night I still want to get my money's worth.

Early replies (first 3)

62450611
Nigga thinks he's in a porn video lmao

62450618
>>62450597
Do you live in brazil or something? Three "high class" escorts in the US would rape you for 4k a piece

62450623
>>62450611
Yeah pretty much lol but keep in mind I'm 40 so I've had my time to think about what it is I really want in life before my time is up and this is one of those. Like, I know it's not going to be like in a porn video I'm not naive but I'm hoping to at least experience some of the acts only possible with three other women.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450626
>>62450618
Not Brazil but also not US. Yeah, I checked and in the US it's quite a bit more expensive but I think pretty much everything is more expensive in the US right now for obvious reasons lol.

62450640
>>62450626
Go ahead bro but honestly you'd have more fun just fucking their holes all night one by one instead of having some awkward foresome where you coom in 15 minutes then sit there with 3 coke addicted whores sitting in your place making small talk

item.title: Extra Cash Besides a JOB — 18 replies — 5.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Extra Cash Besides a JOB

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-07-06 10:24 UTC

OP

Getting the obvious out of the way, everyone on this board is employed or in massive leveraged debt
That aside, what are you tried and true, consistent, easily accessible ways to make money on the side. Whether it's 50 bucks a week or 500 I don't care, just share some examples and provide actual explanations / results of the side gig whenever possible.
We are all on the same money making team bros let's help each other out

Early replies (first 3)

62446864
>>62446849
I fuck around on oil futures. Make like $25/day on average. It's time consuming but I do it during downtime at the office so why not.

62446865
Start a business and work at it 3 years you will be millionaire

62446869
>>62446865
what part of side or extra do you not understand dumb retarded faggot

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62446903
>>62446869
he's right though
most side-gigs are just bad businesses

if you get a successful side-gig you're running a good business.

anyways my side gig was crime scene cleanup, hazardous waste cleanup, and office cleaning. I made millions doing it and now it's my main business.

62446919
>>62446869
You are missing the part where stop crying on incel threads and start a business

62446925
Donate plasma, bonus it filters out microplastics; downside it takes two fucking hours.

62446939
i started a massage business and i only take clients if they sound hot...so far ive been able to make a good call 51% of the time every time.

62446991
>>62446903
See now im imagining dave ramsey telling people to buy a power washer and clean up crime scenes
anyway why is a multimillionaire on 4chan cmon

62446993
>>62446991
To show you losers the way. What you think millionaires don't shit!

62446997
>>62446991
in my generation millionaire isn't anything special. The median net of people my age is almost half a million, meaning at least a third of us are millionaires. Mostly because we bought houses way back in the late 1900's

anyways crime scene cleanup is pretty specialized. It's not regular work for most people because there just aren't that many murders and suicides. But it pays hundreds of dollars per hour so it's worth doing if you can.

62447037
Do you have any of those stores that buy and sell returned Amazon goods near you? Go on a restock day with a barcode scanner app handy and pick up any fancy electronics. It's pretty easy to find 50$+ things like headphones, watches, and more niche devices to resell on marketplace, eBay, etc.

62447512
>>62446849
unironically buy pokemon sealed products and sell in 1-2 years for 3-5x

62447559
>>62447512
Pist a picture of the ones you bought for this opportunity

62447924
anyone know of good AI strats

62449474
Bump

62449570
>>62446991

62449661
>>62447924
Lots. Finding the energy to do it after doing similar shit all day at a full time job is a different story.

Thinking about just running a POD node on my 5090 to rent out inference, but no idea what the returns on it would be, just know that POD's been climbing.

62450354
>>62449661
Yeah that’s what I’ve experienced I’ve made some money but it takes too much active work
